steps.title: Understand what it means for a graph to pass through a point
steps.content: If the graph of $y = f(x)$ passes through the point $(8, 9)$, then the point $(8, 9)$ lies on the curve. For any point $(a, b)$ on the graph of $y = f(x)$, we have $f(a) = b$.

steps.title: Apply this relationship to find f(8)
steps.content: Since $(8, 9)$ is on the graph, we substitute $a = 8$ and $b = 9$ into the relationship $f(a) = b$ to get $f(8) = 9$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
